428 J Street o Suite 620 . Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 3224886
Jlune 17,20A5
Suzanne Jackson, Elections Assistant
County of Santa Barbara
Recorder and Assessor
Post Office Box 159
Santa B arb ar a, C A 93 I 02-0 I 59
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. A-05-116
Dear Ms. Jackson:
This letter is in response to your request on behalf of the Lompoc Healthcare
District regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the..Act',).I
QUESTTON
May committees involved in a ballot measure election to be held on
September 13,2005, combine the semi-annual statement with the first pre-election
statement due on August 4,2005?
CONCLUSION
In connection with a September 13,2005, ballot measure election, committees
may use a filing schedule that combines the semi-annual campaign statement with the
first pre-election statement due on August 4,2005.
I Government Code sections 81000 - g1}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
\.:


File No. 4-05-116
Page No. 2
ANALYSIS
During an election, candidates and committees involved in the elestion are
required to file two campaign statements disclosing contributions received and
expenditures made in connection with that election. These pre-election cmrpaign
statements are generally filed 40 days before the election and again 12 daylef:ore the
election. (Section 8420A.8.) Semi-annual statements are filed Jarnrary 3l and July 31,
respectively. (Section 84200.)
There is a mechanism in the Act that permits the combining of statements when
an overlapping reporting period occurs, or the filing of the semi-annual staternent would
be an additional, unnecessary filing to the two pre-election staternents. (Section 84205.)
In this casg filing the two pre-election statements in connection with a
September 13,2005, election will satisfrthe semi-annual filing requirement.
A campaign statement filing schedule which outlines the due dates for filing
statements in connection with the election is enclosed for your conveniene.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)-
322-s660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
